claim: Hand A's non-herbal content (pharmaceutical + recipes + text-only sections) exhibits the same vowel harmony signature Hand B shows. If confirmed, the apparent typological divergence between hands (H-BV-HAND-B-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 CONFIRMED vs H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 REFUTED on Hand A) is sample-composition masking — Hand A's 86%-herbal sample drowned the harmony signal that emerges from non-herbal content.
evidence_for: Hand A's all-folio test (H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01) was REFUTED at silhouette 0.33, just below the 0.35 threshold. The signal was weakly absent; possible that herbal content was diluting a signal present in non-herbal.

Hand B's harmony confirmation (silhouette 0.65) came from a corpus where herbal is ~39% of content (vs Hand A's 86%). If harmony is content-domain-specific (or content-domain-revealed), Hand A non-herbal should show it.

Methodology lesson 10 (sample-composition parity) directly motivates this test: Hand A and Hand B differed in section composition; Hand A non-herbal subset is the natural composition-controlled comparator.
If Hand A non-herbal CONFIRMS harmony, the manuscript-wide narrative becomes 'one harmonic language sampled differently in each hand' — much cleaner than 'two structurally different systems'.
evidence_against: Hand A non-herbal has only ~19 folios (per H-BV-EXTERNAL-CORRELATION-01: 16 pharma + 2 recipes + 1 text-only). Token count ~2,200-2,500. Eligible stems for vowel harmony test (>=3 distinct vowel-inners + >=5 total) likely 15-30 — well below Hand B's 78 eligible stems.

Low eligible-stem count could produce noisy silhouette regardless of true harmony presence. The locked rule with bootstrap CIs reports both point estimate and confidence interval to reflect this uncertainty.
If sample size is the issue, the test may be inconclusive (silhouette 0.35-0.50 PARTIAL bin) without resolving the question.
If Hand A non-herbal still shows REFUTED silhouette < 0.35, the hands diverge typologically and the v3 narrative needs to handle that genuinely.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Hand A folios (currier_language=='A') partitioned by section.
    NON-HERBAL = folios with section in {pharmaceutical, recipes, text-only} (excludes herbal). Approximately 19 folios.
    HERBAL_CONTROL = folios with section == herbal. Approximately 95 folios.

  TOKENISATION: identical to H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 (MULTI = ['ckh','cth','cph','ch','sh','ol']), N>=3 glyph-units.

  VOWEL HARMONY METHODOLOGY: identical to H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01.
    Eligible stems: >=3 distinct vowel-inner attachments AND >=5 total vowel-inner tokens.
    Feature vectors: 4-dim L1-normalised distribution over {i,e,o,a}.
    K-means k=2, 50 random restarts, Euclidean distance.
    Silhouette score over the clustering.
    Front/back labelling by mean p(i)+p(e).
    Stem-internal vowel prediction accuracy.

  BOOTSTRAP CONFIDENCE INTERVALS:
    For each subset (non-herbal, herbal-control):
      Resample folios with replacement 200 times.
      Recompute eligible stems and silhouette per resample.
      Report 95% CI on silhouette.
    Provides a measure of uncertainty driven by sample size.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    CONFIRMED sample-composition masking: Hand A non-herbal silhouette >= 0.50 AND prediction accuracy >= 0.65
    PARTIAL: silhouette 0.35-0.50, OR (silhouette >= 0.50 AND accuracy < 0.65)
    REFUTED sample-composition masking: silhouette < 0.35
    LOW-POWER flag: <30 eligible stems in non-herbal subset (still report results)

  REPORTING:
    Side-by-side comparison of:
      Hand A all (silhouette 0.3305, REFUTED) — from H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01
      Hand A non-herbal (this test)
      Hand A herbal (this test, control)
      Hand B all (silhouette 0.6495, CONFIRMED) — from H-BV-HAND-B-VOWEL-HARMONY-01
    Plus bootstrap 95% CI on each Hand A subset.
test_metric: Silhouette score and prediction accuracy on Hand A non-herbal vs herbal-only, with bootstrap CIs.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if non-herbal silhouette>=0.50 AND accuracy>=0.65. PARTIAL if 0.35-0.50. REFUTED if <0.35.
tests_remaining: locked_hand_a_non_herbal_harmony_test: vowel harmony on non-herbal Hand A subset + herbal control + bootstrap
reasoning: Cross-hand divergence diagnostic. H-BV-HAND-B-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 confirmed harmony in Hand B (silhouette 0.65); H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 refuted in Hand A (0.33). Two candidate explanations: (a) hands are typologically different (different languages or dialect deep features); (b) sample-composition difference (Hand A 86% herbal masks harmony Hand B's diverse composition reveals). This test isolates (b) by computing harmony on Hand A non-herbal subset only. Outcome is decisive for the v3 narrative.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-18 follow-up to H-BV-HAND-B-FULL-BATTERY-01 STRUCTURALLY_DIFFERENT_SYSTEMS verdict.
